## Summary

Three fixes:

1. **Score badges now use dark solid colors** visible in both web and print-to-PDF views:
   - ≤5: solid red (`bg-red-700 text-white`)
   - 6–7: solid dark amber (`bg-amber-600 text-white`)
   - 8–10: solid dark green (`bg-green-700 text-white`)

2. **Confidence badges use stronger tinted backgrounds** with ring borders for print clarity:
   - High: emerald-200 + emerald ring
   - Moderate: yellow-200 + yellow ring
   - Low: rose-200 + rose ring

3. **"No" character name bug fixed** — the LLM was extracting dialogue fragments (e.g. "No") as character names. Fixed via:
   - Quarantine blocklist of common English words/dialogue fragments
   - Prompt instruction explicitly forbidding dialogue fragment extraction

## Scope

**What this touches:**
- `lib/evaluation/reportCriterionDisplay.ts` — shared score badge colors (affects all pages)
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — confidence badge colors on evaluate page
- `app/reports/[jobId]/page.tsx` — confidence badge colors on reports page
- `components/reports/longform/LongformCriterionAnalyses.tsx` — score text colors + confidence badge colors in expanded criterion analysis
- `app/globals.css` — print CSS: `print-color-adjust: exact` for badge colors, scoped bg-gray-50 reset
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/pass1aQuarantine.ts` — `BLOCKED_CANONICAL_NAMES` blocklist
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/prompts/pass1a-character-sweep.ts` — prompt instruction against dialogue fragment extraction

**What it does NOT touch:**
- PDF/DOCX download route (already uses correct dark RG brand colors)
- Evaluation scoring logic
- Character reducer merge logic (quarantine catches blocked names before reducer sees them)

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- **Score badge colors are now solid/dark** — this is intentional for print visibility. The previous pastel colors (`bg-green-100`) were invisible when browser print-to-PDF strips background colors.
- **Blocked character names**: Only blocks obvious non-names (common English words, pronouns, dialogue interjections). Real character names that happen to be common words (e.g. a character literally named "Grace" or "Will") are multi-syllable proper nouns and are NOT in the blocklist.
- **Print CSS specificity**: The `bg-gray-50` reset now uses `:not([class*="ring-"])` to avoid stripping badge backgrounds. Badge elements use ring classes; non-badge gray backgrounds do not.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — modifies pipeline (Pass 1A quarantine + prompt) but only for character name validation, not scoring.
